title: 2024 Press Releases
summary: This is a listing/index page aggregating all Federal Reserve press releases from calendar year 2024. The page contains dozens of items spanning diverse categories including monetary policy (FOMC minutes, discount rate meetings), enforcement actions, banking applications, regulatory policy, and institutional announcements. No single document or specific macro catalyst is the subject of this source.
key_points: Page is an index of 2024 Fed press releases, not a single document or research piece
Contains FOMC minutes releases from July, September, and November 2024
Includes enforcement actions against multiple financial institutions
Lists banking application approvals for various bank mergers and acquisitions
Contains regulatory policy announcements including stress test framework changes and discount window operational practices
Source is historical (2024) and TODAY (2026-03-31) is approximately 15 months later
why_it_matters: This listing page has no standalone research value. Individual press releases within it (such as FOMC minutes or rate decisions) would be separately archivable, but this index page itself is too broad and thin to serve as a meaningful research record. Users seeking Fed policy information from 2024 should consult specific individual press releases.
important_numbers: $123.5 million (TD Bank fine for AML violations, 10/10/2024)

issues_found: Source is a listing_page per source_type and URL pattern (listing/index of 2024 Fed press releases)
CORE RULE #5 explicitly states: 'If PAGE_TYPE is homepage, navigation_page, listing_page, or search_page — reject'

This page has no standalone archival value; individual FOMC minutes, enforcement actions, and regulatory announcements within it would each warrant separate records
event_type is 'unknown' which is correct for a listing page that spans multiple event types

notes: NON-ARCHIVABLE LISTING PAGE. This Federal Reserve index page contains aggregate links to all 2024 press releases but provides no substantive content itself. Topics span monetary policy, enforcement, regulatory policy, and banking applications. Not directly useful as a research record; individual press releases within this listing would be separately valuable. Historical record—2024 data is 15 months stale relative to TODAY.
